items.html: However, the Yesod and the Malchut in her have no place in ZA [Zeir Anpin] upon which to lean. Indeed, the essence of the making of the Yesod in her was not done like the rest of the Sefirot in her, which were made by the force of ZA's illumination in her; rather, it was made from the discernment of the striking of the Gevurot as they descended from the Tifferet to the Yesod by way of descent. They illuminated there, and their illumination was stamped and engraved there, and from the force of their illumination this Yesod was made. For this reason her Yesod is called "a stamped Hotam [seal]," for it is a seal from the Gevurot that illuminated there by their striking in their descent. The Malchut in her, too, is called a Hotam, for the illumination in her is only from the illumination of the seal of the Yesod. And this is the matter of their saying, "a seal within a seal": the outer seal, which is the Malchut, is drawn and made from the illumination of the inner seal, which is the Yesod in her.
